households.source: ONS, Families and households in the UK, 2025 bulletin

population.source: ONS, Population estimates for the UK, mid-2024

annual_demand_twh.description: UK electricity final consumption by end users (TWh/year), used for the per-MWh-delivered framing. Total demand including losses was 320.2 TWh in 2025; the lower final-consumption figure is used so the per-MWh framing is not overstated by network losses.
annual_demand_twh.source: DESNZ Energy Trends, March 2026 statistical release (2025 annual data, section 5 / table 5.2)

deflator.source: ONS, CPIH Index 00 All Items 2015=100, annual averages (series L522)

bill.source: DESNZ, Digest of UK Energy Statistics (DUKES) 1.3, Sales of electricity and gas by sector

factoids.frame: a year of this subsidy pays
factoids.sentence: A year of direct UK renewable subsidy pays the salaries of 315,000 NHS nurses, in today's money.
factoids.label: NHS nurses' annual salaries paid by one year of direct renewable subsidy, in today's money
factoids.source_name: NHS Employers, Agenda for Change pay scales 2026/27

factoids.frame: the £220bn+ full cost would have built
factoids.sentence: The £220bn+ full cost of subsidising UK renewables, including estimated indirect costs, would have built 559,000 social homes — land included — in today's money.
factoids.label: social homes (land included) the £220bn+ full UK renewables cost would have built, in today's money
factoids.source_name: Cebr for Shelter and the National Housing Federation (2024)

factoids.frame: the £220bn+ full cost would have built
factoids.sentence: The £220bn+ full cost of subsidising UK renewables, including estimated indirect costs, would have built 4 Hinkley Point C-scale nuclear stations in the UK, in today's money.
factoids.label: Hinkley Point C-scale nuclear stations the £220bn+ full UK renewables cost would have built, in today's money
factoids.source_name: New Civil Engineer, reporting EDF's 2025 annual results (20 February 2026)

factoids.frame: the £220bn+ full cost would have built
factoids.sentence: The £220bn+ full cost of subsidising UK renewables would have built 4,400 large new secondary schools, in today's money.
factoids.label: large new secondary schools the £220bn+ full UK renewables cost would have built, in today's money
factoids.source_name: National Audit Office, Condition of school buildings (2023)

factoids.frame: the £220bn+ full cost would have built
factoids.sentence: The £220bn+ full cost of subsidising UK renewables would have built 110 new acute general hospitals, in today's money.
factoids.label: new acute general hospitals the £220bn+ full UK renewables cost would have built, in today's money
factoids.source_name: National Audit Office, Update on the New Hospital Programme (2026)

factoids.frame: the £220bn+ full cost would have built
factoids.sentence: The £220bn+ full cost of subsidising UK renewables would have built 53 Queen Elizabeth-class aircraft carriers, in today's money.
factoids.label: Queen Elizabeth-class aircraft carriers the £220bn+ full UK renewables cost would have built, in today's money
factoids.source_name: National Audit Office, Carrier Strike — the 2012 reversion decision

factoids.frame: the £220bn+ full cost would have built
factoids.sentence: The £220bn+ full cost of subsidising UK renewables would have built 10 Elizabeth Lines — London's Crossrail — in today's money.
factoids.label: Crossrail / Elizabeth Lines the £220bn+ full UK renewables cost would have built, in today's money
factoids.source_name: National Audit Office, Crossrail — a progress update (2021)

factoids.frame: the £220bn+ full cost would have paid for
factoids.sentence: The £220bn+ full cost of subsidising UK renewables would have paid for 18 London 2012 Olympic Games, in today's money.
factoids.label: London 2012 Olympic Games the £220bn+ full UK renewables cost would have paid for, in today's money
factoids.source_name: National Audit Office, The London 2012 Olympic and Paralympic Games — post-Games review

factoids.frame: the £220bn+ full cost would have built
factoids.sentence: The £220bn+ full cost of subsidising UK renewables would have built 220 billion-pound football stadiums, in today's money.
factoids.label: billion-pound football stadiums the £220bn+ full UK renewables cost would have built, in today's money
factoids.source_name: Tottenham Hotspur Stadium reported build cost (Wikipedia)

factoids.frame: a year of this subsidy pays
factoids.sentence: A year of UK renewable subsidy pays the salaries of 241,000 experienced schoolteachers, in today's money.
factoids.label: experienced schoolteachers' annual salaries paid by one year of UK renewable subsidy, in today's money
factoids.source_name: NASUWT, School teachers' pay scales England — upper pay range UPS3 (2025-26)

factoids.frame: a year of this subsidy pays
factoids.sentence: A year of UK renewable subsidy pays the salaries of 255,000 police officers, in today's money.
factoids.label: police officers' annual salaries paid by one year of UK renewable subsidy, in today's money
factoids.source_name: PolicePay, police constable pay scales 2025-26 (top of scale, PP7)

factoids.figure_label: children fed at school for a year
factoids.frame: a year of this subsidy could give free school meals to
factoids.sentence: A year of UK renewable subsidy could give free school meals to 24,400,000 children for a year, in today's money.
factoids.label: children given free school meals for a year by one year of UK renewable subsidy, in today's money
factoids.source_name: DfE, free school meals expansion grant methodology — £505 per pupil per annum (2026-27)
